turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: We'll hear argument next in Number oh oh seventeen thirty-seven, The Watchtower Bible and Tract Society of New York, versus The Village of Stratton, Ohio. Mr. Polidoro. [Inaudible]

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Mr. Chief Justice and may it please the Court It's eleven o'clock Saturday morning in the Village of Stratton. Good morning. In light of recent events, I've made a special effort to come to your door to speak to you about what the Prophet Isaiah has referred to as the good news of something better. That's the good news Christ Jesus spoke about, the good news of the Kingdom of God. It is a criminal act to go from door to door in the Village of Stratton and deliver that message unless one has first obtained a permit from the village to do so. It is also illegal to go from door to door and hand out a leaflet that says, democracy is wonderful, please vote next week. In drafting a permit scheme designed to address fraud and privacy that includes those interested in pure discourse, Stratton has eliminated the ability to engage in door to door, one on one political advocacy. As such, we submit the ordinance is overbroad and unconstitutional.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: I I don't see how it has eliminated any chance of a political dis- of going door to door in a political discourse if if you register with the with the mayor and th- you can't be turned down if you fill out that form. You can go door to door, except where there's a no trespassing sign. [Inaudible]

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Your Honor, we are not s- suggesting at all that a resident cannot post a no trespassing sign and keep all away from their door. However, in order to get the right to go to that door, one must go to the mayor, give his name, give a brief description of the cause they want to speak about, as well as their association or affiliation. We believe that this Court in McIntyre said there's an interest in having anonymous works enter the marketplace of ideas, and that unquestionably outweighs any interest in requiring disclosure as a precondition to entry. [Inaudible]

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: Well, i- th- th- th- do you focus on the right of anonymity, or the the right of unrestricted speech, as of the time you get to the door, or when you go to the mayor's office, because our most recent cases talk about going to the door, and that's where your brief focuses on. It seems to me that what you've begun with, and this is also troubling to me, is that you have to make the disclosure to the mayor as well, those ar- so I take it those are two different parts of your case. [Inaudible]

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Correct, Justice Kennedy.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: But you emphasize only the latter, really, in your briefs. Are are the records that the mayor keeps, are they open to the public?

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: They are, absolutely, indeed, Your Honor. Under Ohio State law those records are open to the public, so anyone would be able to go to look at the record, which would include the name, your message,

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: and your associations. So you you you you you would have...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: have no objection to this statute if if you didn't have to give your name?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Your Honor, we...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: to say, you can't talk to anybody without getting a permit, so long as you don't have to give your name when you get the permit?

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Your Honor, we believe the name is the constitutional infirmity in deed.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: That that's the only problem, so if if this were revised somehow so that all you had to give was a reference, maybe somebody in the community who would vouch for the integrity of these Jehovah's Witnesses, that would then be okay,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: because they wouldn't have to -- The only problem...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: is if they have to give their name.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: No, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: I think that's the stronger part of your case.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Well but y- i-

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: you call them neighbors, but I take it in many cases they're simply strangers who come into town.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Well, actually, Your Honor, in this circumstance the Village of Wellsville, which is a neighboring village, has assumed responsibility for preaching door to door in the Village of Stratton, and many individuals are, in fact, known to each other, and that's common in most areas of this country.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Can you tell me why that...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: is? I I I agree that at some level it seems contrary to our traditions, but if we can focus just on going to the mayor's office, it seems to me that one of the strong- parts of your case is did you have to identify your cause.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Your Honor, Your Honor Jehovah's Witnesses have a long historical memory as to what they've suffered at the hands of...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: Well...

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: purely to go door to door to speak the good news.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: What's wrong with identifying the cause?

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Your Honor, we believe that under this Court's holding there are some individuals who do not want to identify their cause.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: So...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: take it your argument only goes to the political speech, d- d- because I unders- as I understand it, Jehovah's Witnesses do not object to identifying themselves, and and and your concern is with the overbreadth that that goes into the political sphere, is that correct?

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: That is absolutely correct, Justice Souter.

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: And yet the the question that you phrase says that this permit, which contains one's name, now, when we turn to the village's brief, they say, no, it doesn't.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Justice Ginsburg, if I might, at the joint appendix on page two forty-eight A is a copy of the blank permit and it says, permit to canvass, to solicit, et cetera, and it says, in accordance with the relevant provision of the statute, the mayor of the Village of Stratton, Ohio, has issued to the above applicant a permit the above applicant a permit, so we believe on the plain language of the permit, the name is, indeed,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Did you ever  did you ever apply for a permit? So you don't know, as a...

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: as a matter of practical fact, how that would work out?

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: That's correct,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: with respect to Jehovah's Witnesses.

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: applicant number five.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: There's no showing, Justice Souter, in the ordinance that there is any sort of numbering scheme.

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: Well, no, there's no there's that's the trouble, I mean, I think, with the argument.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: But Justice Souter, I believe the exhibit would be inconsistent with the concession of the village that name disclosure is, in fact, required at the door.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: They said about...

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: five times in their brief that the permit that must be shown to the police officer or the homeowner does not have a name on it does not have a name on it.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: It's different for two reasons, Justice Ginsburg.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: I thought you were...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: saying it only applied to to to those who were engaged in the political I thought that was th- the the point of your overbreadth objection.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: I think there's a distinction, Justice Souter, to just pure speech, the dissemination of information and ideas, and speech that in some way involves the mechanics of the electoral process.

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: But one of your examples was that door to door solicitation of signatures for inti- an initiative to put on the ballot, and that's exactly what was involved in the Buckley case.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Yes, Justice Ginsburg, but that disclosure took place after the one on one dissemination of information was done.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: She couldn't have -- could not have...

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: have been a circulator, then, because a condition of being a circulator she could not have submitted her petitions, because to submit the petition she has to have this affidavit, and I don't see I really can't see any difference in consequence whether the official form is made before or made after.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Because of the compelling governmental governmental interest that's involved, Justice Ginsburg, and that's the distinction.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: one on one...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: That doesn't handle your example.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: the example. It's not worth your time. You shouldn't have used the example...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: of a petition circulator.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: That's correct,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: If that's -- is that...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: case?

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: It is, indeed, the...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Is it the that...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: Thank you is is it the the case that your clients don't ask for any money, not a penny, and they don't sell Bibles, and they're not selling anything, they're all all that they do is say, I want to talk to you about religion? They don't ask for anything.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Your Honor, the record is absolutely clear, in the Village of Stratton, Jehovah's Witnesses did not ask for money.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Don't you hand out that...

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: the Watchtower, too?

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: We do, Your Honor, on a no-cost basis if someone's interested in reading it.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: So what about your...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: you said just you briefly mentioned because I, you know, I've had many fine conversations with Jehovah's Witnesses.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: No, Your Honor, Jehovah's Witnesses have no objection to giving their name.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: speech.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: you would say that w- w- w- w- without reference to the name.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: That's correct, Your Honor.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: That is that is that here in front of us? I mean, what I'm having a hard time getting my hands on is, it looks as if a group of people who really don't mind giving their name are objecting in the name of a different group of people who might not, and those might be people who might want to say, vote for Smith, and what's worrying me about deciding this case in the context which is obvious from the briefs is, that might have considerable legal implications for the question of whether you can require disclosure of campaign contributions, which is a totally different issue, but would seem legally related, so I'd I'd appreciate those three things that I've mentioned or y- you see what I'm what i- what the blur is in my mind, and I'd appreciate your views on that.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Justice Breyer, it has been our contention that the ordinance was drafted in an overbroad manner, meaning reaching speech that was not the source of harm, including our religious speech, but also including pure political speech.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Of Of course, to do that we would just say, you can't require the name to be disclosed.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Your Honor, we then don't see...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Have your...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: photograph taken, maybe, you know.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: That's what you want us to do?

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: No, not at all.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: believe...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: think so.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: We don't believe that this Court should sanction a regulation of a government that requires one citizen to get a license to speak to another citizen at that citizen's home.

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: Okay, but the basis I understand the nub of your argument is is goes back to your answer to Justice Ginsburg, and that i- in in distinguishing Buckley, and I think what you were saying was, in Buckley the government had an interest because it had an interest in checking the veracity of the of of the the the the the endorsement solicitat- the petition solicitation process, are these real people, go to the person who asked and check, whereas here, if we're not talking about the solicitation of money and of signatures in in a political sphere, there's nothing for the government to verify, and therefore there is no interest here, whereas there was an interest in Buckley, and that's the distinction.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Precisely. Precisely, Justice Souter.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: The brief didn't...

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: mention i- you you took Buckley as being a hundred percent in your favor, and that what surprised me, is that Buckley made the very distinction that you say is inappropriate here, that is, face to face encounter, you don't have to have your name, but back in in an official's office, you do.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Your Honor, I I think that Buckley followed on language in Your Honor's portion of the opinion in McIntyre, where you spoke to limited circumst- there might be larger circumstances where identification might be required, and in the electoral process, the mechanics of how an election works, those are larger purposes.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: You...

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: have any case in the most of the Jehovah [: Jehovah's] Witnesses cases dealt with that official who was exercising discretion to keep out the Witnesses but letting in the Republicans, and that that was that line of cases.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Justice Ginsburg, the only precedent that I'm aware of with respect to identification schemes comes from the dicta in Cantwell and Martin that speaks about a municipality, a z- municipality's interest in knowing strangers in the community when connected with the solicitation of funds.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Don't you...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: think that the practice around the country of various towns and cities is to require permits for people to go door to door in residential areas? I think I even live in a community that probably does that.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Justice...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: they want to identify the people who are going door to door.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Justice O'Connor, my experience is quite to the opposite, that after the battles between Jehovah's Witnesses and the municipalities in the thirties and forties, municipalities have stopped attempting to regulate Jehovah's Witnesses by forcing them to get a permit to go from door to door.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Well,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: there...

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: there there was a story in the in the paper just last week here about the the double murder up at Dartmouth, how the kids who did it went from house to house purporting to be taking a survey, and then what they were actually doing was casing the houses for robbery, so th- that's certainly something that a municipality can take into consideration, isn't it?

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Chief Justice Rehnquist, we think it's implausible to think that someone intent on committing a violent crime would stop at the city hall to get a permit or in any way be deterred, and in the circumstance Your Honor posits of someone just going door to door and observing, or taking notes, the ordinance in this instance doesn't prevent that.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: comes into play is if you speak...

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: it it does give a give a record of who it was, which m- would probably deter someone.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: W- I think you're wrong when you said,

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: i- only if they speak, that the the mayor requires anybody who rings door to door.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: No, Chief Jus- no, Justice Ginsburg, I believe it's anyone who wants to go to door to door to discuss a cause, and that's the language of the ordinance.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Now, suppo-

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: suppose the  --

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: that.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Which is a speech interest, Justice O'Connor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: But but suppose I am concerned...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: that the that the mayor, in my view, or the the the Government has no right to demand f- previous registration or to give his consent before I go to my neighbors to talk about neighborhood problems, or whatever.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Excuse me,

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Justice Kennedy, if I might...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: What's your...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: best case, or best what are your best cases for the proposition that it is simply unconstitutional for the government to require a permit before you go door to door to talk about a cause? What are your best...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: cases?

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: predicated on Lovell, predicated on Schneider.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Most of...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: those most of those cases I I I think they're the closest, too, but they're not right on point, because they were concerned with the discretion...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Justice Kennedy there...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: the official had...

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: there is discretion in this instance.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: But a- and...

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: also may I say there's language in those cases that suggest that it it would be th- i- you read it yourself, this stranger has to identify himself or herself.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: I- if I if I might, Justice Ginsburg and Justice Kennedy, when we look at the ordinance on appendix four A, and it's section one sixteen oh three B six, it says that the mayor can ask for such other...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Four four...

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: A of the petition?

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Of I'm sorry, it's our brief for petitioners, Chief Justice Rehnquist, page four A, section B six, such other information concerning the registrant and its business or purpose as may be reasonably necessary to accurately describe the nature of the privilege desired.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: But that's -- that has been...

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: taken out of the case.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: No.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: I thought that...

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: the the only thing that the mayor asks isn't wasn't there something in the district court opinion that cut that back?

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: No, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: But I thought...

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: the village made a concession.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: concession in some court?

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: not at all.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: I'll ask...

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: the village if they did, because I remember something that said we're going to ask only for the name and the address.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: No, it's...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: If even if that's so, I mean, where I slight state of confusion is, I thought this case now sounds very theoretical.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Mr. Polidoro, I'm...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: I'm confused here.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Exactly, yes.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: that you do w- not want to have to go to city hall in order to ring...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: doorbells? And n- no matter what?

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: They want you there because they know at least you'll find out which homeowners don't want people around, and you'll l- read what the rules are, all right.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Justice Breyer, we have not contested that provision of the ordinance, section one sixteen oh seven, which allows a homeowner to post a no-solicitation sign and then go to the village and say, I want this group but not this group, has not been contested.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: But your position is, of course, that governments always want to know what its people are thinking, but they don't have any right to find out.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Well, that's i- that's il- an expression of the residents' interest in who they want.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: I'm talking about your clients.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Your Honor,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: we...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Take it.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Okay.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: I think that's an appropriate point Chief Justice Rehnquist asked.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Very well, Mr.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Polidoro.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Mr. Cantor, we'll hear from you.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: Mr. Chief Justice, and may it please the Court.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Do you know...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: any other any other case of ours that has even involved an ordinance of this breadth, that involves solicitation, not asking for money, not selling goods, but even, you know, I want to talk about Jesus Christ, or I want to talk about about protecting the environment? no- di- i- Is there have we had a case like...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: There is --

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: fact that i- that I'm unfamiliar with something like maybe they've become current now, but the mere fact that I don't even know of such cases, over two centuries, makes me...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: You haven't been around that long.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: I d- I d-

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: do do you know of any?

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: Your Honor, there's no holding by this Court that identifies...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: this --

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: a holding, a case.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: There is no case that identifies a breadth of this case, but perhaps that might be part of the beauty, beauty in the sense that it is it is content-neutral,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: and that is --

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: You you think it's it's it's a beautiful idea that I have to ask the government for permission before I go down the block, where I don't know all of the people, I wave to them but I don't really know them, I say, I want to talk to you because I'm concerned about the garbage pick-up, because I'm concerned about our congressman, whatever.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: No, Your Honor --

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: No, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Well, how about...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: trick-or-treaters? Do they have to go get a permit?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Interesting...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: ordinance it looks like it, doesn't it?

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: Your Honor...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: And they're soliciting, too.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: They are.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: The answer is no, and...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Why not? Literally it...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: does.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: The track rec- i- the track...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: You may not...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: enforce it, but it literally covers them, doesn't it?

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: That hypothetical was raised by the trial court judge, Your Honor,

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: Mhm.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: and it was answered by Mr. Bruzzese to the to the negative, the reason why it was answered to the negative is that these people are not who are going door to door are not seeking u- to communicate, they are s- they are begging candy, and...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: therefore --

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: on it did have it...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: Or or Christmas carollers?

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: Sale sale would would constitute a conduct that would...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: require...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Why does...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: This is really --

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: from your neighbor? Do I have to get a permit to go borrow a cup of sugar from my neighbor?

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: No, Your Honor, I don't believe that...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Th- th- this is really wh- b- b- before...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: This is really a novel argument.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: It's only communication...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: that we're concerned about.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: The limiting language, Your Honor, deals with canvassing and hawking of the u- of the area.

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: Okay, go back to Justice Kennedy's e- example that started all of this.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: No, Your Honor.

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: Why?

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: Because it is not the type of cause that u- that the communication is...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: directed at.

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: who knows? I mean, where where's the restrictive definition of cause?

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: Within the beginning of the ordinance.

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: And and what're the what's the language that you rely on?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: No canvas or solicit --

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: I'm looking at section one sixteen point zero three.

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: Three A, okay.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Also petitioner's page three...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: Okay, now ju- tell me where you're reading from, and let's go...

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: No canvasser, solicitor, peddlar, hawker, itinerant merchant, or transient vendor of merchandise or services who is described in one sixteen point zero one of this chapter, and who intends to go o- in or upon private property, or a private resident of the village, for any other purposes described in point zero one, shall go in or upon such private property or residence without first registering.

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: Okay.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: same Yeah I mean, un- unless you're interpreting canvasser very literally, in which case it wouldn't include Jehovah's Witnesses why are Jehovah's Witnesses covered? Are are they canvassers, solicitors, peddlers, hawkers, itinerant merchants or transient vendors of merchandise or services?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: [Inaudible]

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: They're none of those, are they?

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: The district court determined th- that they were canvassers.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: They were canvassers. So...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: you you have a very broad definition of canvassers if it includes Jehovah's Wit- I I I would have thought a canvasser is just somebody who said, I'm doing a survey, I'm canvassing public opinion, or something like that, but if it's if it includes Jehovah's Witnesses, it certainly includes the the the you know, the the the the garbage u- ca- canvasser, u- so...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: The...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: dictionary...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: says canvasser is a person who's looking for a vote.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: city's purpose is to prevent annoyance...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: the...

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: city of the property owner.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: not...

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: want to be...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: canvassed.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: That is correct, Your Honor, that is not...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: challenged. That is -- that's...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: the registration?

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: The purpose is to prevent th- the p- the purpose of the registration, is the initial registration...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: is...

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: to identify who in the community is going door to door.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: Because?

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: Because of two factors, the f- privacy of the oh person who lives with...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: H- ho- how how does it work? Spell it out.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: I- In Martin versus Struthers, Your Honor, in footnote five, this Court relied upon the utilization of a book as well as an FBI bulletin to determine that those who go door to door may be doing so as a guise.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: It could b- be, and I suppose you could say everybody who gets up on a soap- soapbox may may provoke a riot, but you know, there the- this seems to me one of the normal risks of life that doesn't let the Government go around.

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: That was the provision that I thought was out of it because of the concession that the city made.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Your Honor, that provision remains.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: That provision is a catch-all to determine or make sure that the identifying identifying information is present, the identification o- of the individual as well...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: as the cause.

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: point when the mayor said, all I'm going to ask is the name and the address?

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: The first five sections of that subsection give the information.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: Well, footnote fourteen of Martin has to do with solicitation of funds.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: That's correct, Your Honor.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: That's correct, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: was...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: No.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: you cited...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: when we were talking about whether or not you can just at least give your name, said this is necessary for those who solicit funds, but your ordinance goes much bu- sweeps much more widely than the solicitation of funds, so footnote fourteen doesn't support the ordinance that r- is now before us.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: I'm going to suggest to Your Honor that it does, and the reason that it does is because it I it requires identification information, and that identification information is necessary regardless of whether someone is going to solicit funds, or whether or not he is going to going t- going to go door to door for some other...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: purpose, as a pretext.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: I you know, I'm the the th- that's that's a common common ploy of people who want to commit a crime.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: That that that...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: That minor risk of a o- of a crime occurring is enough to re- to require everybody who wants to ring a doorbell to register at city hall? Of course it isn't.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: U- The determination of the nature and the amount of risk is perhaps highlighted by MacMurray's testimony.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: Well,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: McIntyre...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: suggests that an anonymity requirement makes it content-based.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: Your Honor, the issue the issue in at the registration aspect deals with that we are not at the point of persuasion.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: Well, the disclosure means that it certainly is not anonymous.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: Y- Your Honor, I would suggest that you resolve it based on the record.

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: Well, we're not supposed to be resolve these b- back door questions.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: Your Honor, this case was tried on the registration requirement.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: You mean you...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: you mean, it it originally was that they just didn't want to register, a- anonymous or not?

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: No, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: included within the phraseology --

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: of the of the language.

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: Well, the question we...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: took refers...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: to display upon demand.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: The permit...

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: The display, there is a display of the permit required on demand.

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: One of the things in this record that you you did delete, at one time you gave the homeowners a choice specifically of u- Jehovah Witnesses in or out, right?

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: Yes, Your...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Honor.

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: And that, now you deleted that but could the could the village say to the homeowner, it's your choice, you tell us who you want and you don't want, and we'll enforce that, so if you don't want the Jehovah Witnesses you don't have to have them, you don't want the Democrats you don't have to have them? Do Would that be permissible for the village to do to facilitate the homeowner's choice?

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: The village would be acting as a bulletin board.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: And the village...

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: could it enforce u- that private discrimination?

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: Your Honor, there is no requirement that anyone decide that anyone allow someone else to come into their home w- if whether for a good or a bad purpose.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Does the State...

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: when the State assists that private choice I mean we're not we're talking about limitations on the State, not on the homeowner.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: Well, we are only assisting that u- u- that the u- prior cases that this Court has considered deals with the Government being some type of a censor or limiter of the speech that is going.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Suppose...

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: the homeowner said, only Caucasian solicitors?

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: Your Honor, I believe that that would be invalid on its face.

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: Well how is that any different from no Jehovah Witnesses?

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: Because the issue there is that this is a speech that is not that they do not wish to a- to accept.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: That's very nice.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: Well, the the the issue here, Your Honor, is dealing with whether or not this is a consensual intrusion.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: I take it there are many m- m- m- m- gated, so called private communities where the solicitor can never go in at all.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: I assume that's the...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: the way they work.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: In the Village of Stratton --

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: guess you're just trying to give your residents the protection that people have when they live in closed enclaves.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: That would be appropriate to c-

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Although I don't --

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: I I I haven't seen any cases about those closed enclaves.

turns.name: ABRAHAM CANTOR
turns.text: Thank you, Your Honor.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Thank you, Mr. Cantor.

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: Mr. Chief Justice, and may it please the Court.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: N- not necessarily.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: I mean, you're envisioning little little little cottages with a pathway up to the door.

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: But Your Honor,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Or maybe another house that...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: th- w- the they don't have a setback restriction, the door is right, you know, right on the public public sidewalks, knocks on the door.

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: But the point is, Your Honor, the ordinance is designed to protect the concerns that village residents have, no matter how much space is between them and...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: That's a different point, but I mean, I I don't...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: think you can say that the difference is that this involves only speech that occurs on private property, not necessarily.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: necessarily.

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: Your Honor, the the point would be that when someone knocks on a door, no matter how far away they are from a sidewalk or a street, there is concern o- on the part of the resident that this often a stranger, certainly an uninvited person, is here on my property, perhaps just a few inches away from from a public way, but nonetheless coming to my home, perhaps asking to actually enter my home, and I think the village is entitled to say, we're concerned about that kind of...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: activity.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: you're concerned about people who were even not concerned about it.

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: But Your Honor, that's no different than the Frisby case decided by this Court fourteen years ago.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Well, we have...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: we have always said that that picketing is a a activity which i- which is focused, it's constant, it repeats a message over a long period of time, and that's that's simply different from a a single encounter, which is what you prevent.

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: Nonetheless, I think in both situations, Your Honor, the Government is rightly concerned about an activity that causes some public problem.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: But that...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: is met, as has already been pointed out, by the opportunity to put up a no trespassing, no solicitor sign.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: It addresses --

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: ordinance have to do more?

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: Sure, well, I certainly no trespassing signs address some of the...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: problem, but those...

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: often provide after the fact remedies, if someone actually invades the space you can prosecute, but by then you may well not be able to find the person any more, and even with the the no solicitation registration form that the petitioners have not challenged, that does not protect the person who, for whatever reason, chooses not to put up such a sign.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: What --

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: what standard of review should we apply, do you suppose, in looking at this ordinance?

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: Well, I think that both of the courts below applied applied the proper approach.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Well, that would...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: be intermediate scrutiny?

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: That's what the court of appeals...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: And under that...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: standard, aren't you re- isn't the Government required to show that it restricts no more speech than is reasonably necessary to accomplish its interests?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: That's right.

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: Yes, Your Honor, e- and I think that's true here, because every time that someone approaches the private property, comes onto the private property of a village resident, those residents are rightly concerned about who this uninvited...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: person is, and what are they going --

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: you just print up some signs that people can put up if they want saying, no no canvassers without a permit.

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: But I think the village rightly is concerned with having some information...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: N- n-

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: Given, say, the imaginary signs, what is the purpose? What is the purpose f- to require them just to come down to the mayor?

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: Because it it gives some record o- on file about who these people are, thereby giving village residents a little greater sense of security that if something goes wrong when this person is on my land, I'm not...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: And I I s- suppose...

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: If the person turns out to be a con man, too, it would e- enable a better chance of locating him to make...

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: Absolutely.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: clear, I think.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: of a con man who doesn't want any money or anything else?

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: Oh I i- mean just coming...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: We're serious.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: into your house...

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: maybe maybe he wants to steal your property.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: Potential thief, a ga- g-

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Sure.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: A potential...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: thief who is willing to to rape and and burgle, but is but stops short of failing to register at city hall, right?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: i- Your Honor --

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: gives your citizens great confidence that laugh>

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: But, Your Honor, I think the fact that the ordinance is not going to catch everyone who's going to do something wrong doesn't mean that the village is not entitled to try to do something to give its residents some comfort.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: The question is, how much?

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: How necessary is it? We we can all stipulate that the safest societies in the world are totalitarian dictatorships.

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: I I think it does Your Honor, and it's it's a very modest restriction.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: seems to me much different than a totali- --

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: find a single case reporting a single o- i- municipality that has ever enacted an ordinance of that type.

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: Well, I but I think, Your Honor, it's a it shows that this village is perhaps trying to to take some proactive steps that other communities have not...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Do we know where the village...

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: got it from? I I don't think it was original with this village, was it?

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: i- I'm not sure.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: The the the mayor here said I i- in his testimony it's on three eighty-five A of the appe- I nev- I've never I I never have denied anybody a permit.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: It it's extraordinary...

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: I think he was s- speaking about the Jehovah's Witnesses, Your Honor.

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: Could the city say, to facilitate the homeowners' choice, we're going to say, nobody rings bells for anything, but the homeowner the home make the homeowner the one to come to sign up in city hall to say, I don't mind having solicitors?

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: i- Certainly that's the part of the ordinance that the petitioners have not challenged here.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: But suppose,

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: j- in in order to meet the objection that the solicitors don't want to sign up, or the canvassers don't want to sign up, the city says, well, i- we'll just i- sh- have a total ban on ringing doorbells, but any homeowner who wants to have it can?

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: I mean, given your the the Court's decision in Martin, I wouldd be hesitant to say you can have an outright ban on ringing doorbells or knocking.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: Thank you, Mr. Gormley.

turns.name: DAVID M. GORMLEY
turns.text: Thank you Your Honor.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Mr. Polidoro, you have four minutes.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: Thank you, Mister Chief Justice.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: May I ask, if you're through...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: i- as I looked at page one ninety-nine and I thought that was a list of exceptions to the to the ordinance.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: It is, and what...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: So that in other words the trick-or-treaters are expressly excepted, just like Christmas carollers and the others...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Well, what...

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: had happened there, Justice Stevens, they would be accepted if the box was checked and they were allowed, and that particular form, no one was allowed at the door.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: You mean the the person applying for the ordinance has to check all these things to be...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: I don't quite understand how it --

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: No, what --

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: who allows certain solicitations.

turns.name: PAUL D. POLIDORO
turns.text: That's the homeowner's for-.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Thank you, Mr. Polidoro. The case is submitted.

turns.name: THE MARSHAL
turns.text: The honorable court is now adjourned until tomorrow at ten o'clock.
